reference: HEC-RAS 6.x bridge high-flow methodology (FHWA sluice Fig. 5-5, submerged orifice, Bradley weir, energy fallback). Golden headwaters generated from STREAM-1D Phase 4.2 solver and cross-checked against discharge balance at the solved WSEL.

cases.name: sluice_gate_free_tailwater
cases.notes: 10 m rectangular channel, z=0, Q=200 m³/s, TW=2.5 m (< low chord 5 m). Tailwater below deck → FHWA sluice-gate pressure path; reconciled from low-flow energy when EGL > low chord.

cases.name: submerged_orifice_high_tailwater
cases.notes: TW=5.4 m (≥ max low chord on uniform deck). Submerged orifice Q = C A_net √(2g(E_up − TW)); no weir (EGL below crest).

cases.name: low_flow_reconcile_to_pressure
cases.notes: TW=4.5 m, Q=200 m³/s. Class A/B low-flow EGL exceeds max low chord → governing headwater from pressure opening (sluice), not low-flow WSEL alone.

cases.name: combined_pressure_and_weir
cases.notes: TW=5.5 m, Q=280 m³/s, uniform deck 5–7 m. Combined balance Q_opening + Q_weir; segment EGL clears crest.

cases.name: crowned_deck_segment_weir
cases.notes: Piecewise crest: high chord 6.5 m over center 5 m, 7.0 m at right abutment. Q=220 m³/s — partial segment overtopping before global min crest WSEL (Phase 4.2 segment weir).

cases.name: explicit_energy_high_flow
cases.notes: bridge_high_flow_methods=1 (energy through opening). TW=5.6 m above low chord; bypasses pressure/weir split — HEC-RAS energy high-flow option.
